etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 今(今) (jīn) -> contains in one's mouth
etymology.interpretation: 含(hán, "contain") combines 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") as the semantic component indicating the action related to the mouth, with 今(今) (jīn), which provides the sound. The sense of "containing" arises from the image of holding something within the mouth, implying retention or keeping inside.

examples.chinese: 这瓶水含有丰富的矿物质(這瓶水含有豐富的礦物質)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè píng shuǐ hányǒu fēngfù de kuàngwùzhì.
examples.english: This bottle of water contains abundant minerals.

examples.chinese: 他的信含有重要的信息(他的信含有重要的信息)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xìn hányǒu zhòngyào de xìnxī.

examples.chinese: 这本书含有丰富的历史知识(這本書含有豐富的歷史知識)，适合喜欢研究历史的人(適合喜歡研究歷史的人)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū hányǒu fēngfù de lìshǐ zhīshi, shìhé xǐhuān yánjiū lìshǐ de rén.
examples.english: This book contains rich historical knowledge, suitable for those who like to study history.
